etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 契(契) (qì)
etymology.interpretation: The character 喫 (chī, "to eat") is composed of the semantic component 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating the action is related to the mouth, and the phonetic component 契(契) (qì), suggesting the sound. The choice of 契(契) as a phonetic element was based on its phonological match rather than semantic relevance, guiding the pronunciation in the context of using the mouth—fundamentally linked to acts like eating.
etymology.simplification: The character 喫 was simplified to 吃 by replacing 契(契) (qì) with 乞(乞) (qǐ), retaining the mouth radical to preserve the core semantic connection related to eating.

examples.chinese: 每天我都会吃水果(每天我都會吃水果)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān wǒ dōu huì chī shuǐguǒ.

examples.chinese: 他在餐厅里吃午饭(他在餐廳裡吃午飯)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài cāntīng lǐ chī wǔfàn.
examples.english: He eats lunch in the restaurant.

examples.chinese: 如果你饿了(如果你餓了)，就吃这些饼干(就吃這些餅乾)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ è le, jiù chī zhèxiē bǐnggān.
examples.english: If you are hungry, eat these cookies.
